items.headline: US Markets Closed for Juneteenth; Futures Mixed After Hawkish Fed Stance and Strong Consumer Data
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are closed today for the Juneteenth holiday. Heading into the long weekend, investors were balancing robust consumer data, including a 0.9% rise in May retail sales and a 3.8% climb in pending home sales, against a more hawkish Federal Reserve outlook. While the Fed kept rates unchanged, recent projections signal a potential rate hike by year-end, driven by persistent inflation concerns. Geopolitical optimism from the US-Iran peace agreement was somewhat tempered by a delay in follow-up talks on nuclear negotiations.

items.statContext: +1.1% on Thursday, June 18. S&P 500 futures slipped 0.2% on Friday morning.
+1.91% on Thursday, June 18. Nasdaq-100 futures were up approximately 1.5% on Friday morning.
+0.14% on Thursday, June 18. Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were less than 0.1% lower on Friday morning.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Signals Potential Rate Hikes by Year-End
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held its key interest rate unchanged but its latest projections indicate that more officials expect at least one rate hike by the end of 2026. Under new Chairman Kevin Warsh, the median fed funds rate forecast rose to 3.8% for 2026, up from 3.4%, reflecting growing concerns over persistent inflation.

items.headline: Strong Consumer Spending and Manufacturing Data
items.summary: Recent economic data showed resilience in consumer spending, with US retail sales rising 0.9% in May and pending home sales climbing 3.8%. The Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing index also improved to 10.3 in June, with new orders surging, although rising prices paid component reinforced inflation concerns.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Gains Driven by AI and Chip Demand
items.summary: The technology sector led market gains on Thursday, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index surging 6.4%. Companies like Intel, Nvidia, and Micron Technology saw significant boosts due to AI memory strength and a new chip-making partnership between Intel and Apple.

items.headline: Energy Sector Faces Headwinds Amid Easing Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: While geopolitical tensions eased with the US-Iran peace agreement and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the energy sector faced downward pressure from lower oil prices. This contributed to energy and precious metals sectors lagging behind, with broad losses across commodities.

items.headline: Kroger Reports Q1 2026 Earnings
items.summary: Kroger Co. ($KR) reported its Q1 2026 earnings before the market open today, June 19, 2026. The company’s weaker profit guidance on Thursday contributed to its stock decline.
